council.name: The Strategist
council.role: Big picture, revenue opportunities, market positioning

memos.title: R&D Council Session
memos.summary: The council converged on a foundation-first agenda: establish canonical sources of truth, reduce operator fragility, and build a decision-oriented operating pulse before chasing broader monetization. DILESA remains strategically important, but only through staged, low-regret migration steps. Trusted business metrics, especially for COAGAN recovery, should feed a weekly operating cadence rather than exist as passive dashboards.

ideas.title: Canonical Source Treaty + Data Access Layer
ideas.summary: Establish a unified source-of-truth program: typed adapters, app-visible source registry, explicit ownership and sync rules, and reconciliation logs for dual-source entities. This creates the trust layer required for both product and operating decisions.

ideas.title: Operating Pulse — What Needs Beto Today?
ideas.summary: Create a cross-business operating layer that ranks the 3-7 actions or decisions that deserve attention today, using KPI exceptions, infra health, calendar/travel context, DILESA risk, COAGAN recovery, and AI operations. It should reduce cognitive load rather than expand dashboards.

ideas.title: DILESA Clientes Split + 3-Layer ERP Completion
ideas.summary: Continue DILESA's ERP transformation, but through a staged, rollback-heavy migration centered on the Clientes split, Expediente extraction, and eventual 3-layer completion. Use practical access-pattern cuts rather than pursuing schema purity for its own sake.

ideas.title: Solo-Operator Reliability Layer
ideas.summary: Build the survivability stack for BSOP: release guardrails, operator scripts, degraded-mode runbooks, and a stress/load-management policy. The goal is not elegant infrastructure but dependable operation for a single human with AI support.

ideas.title: Trustworthy Metrics Layer for COAGAN and Cross-Business Review
ideas.summary: Start with a COAGAN tree-loss and recovery model, then build a narrow KPI layer across businesses that supports weekly review, funding, and escalation decisions. Metrics should be trusted, sparse, and action-linked.

highlights.speaker: synthesizer
highlights.message: Three separate ideas converged into one dominant thesis: canonical data truth must be explicit in both documentation and code before BSOP scales safely.

highlights.speaker: synthesizer
highlights.message: The council rejected a false choice between metrics and judgment: dashboards only survive if they feed an operating pulse that changes what Beto reviews or funds this week.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: DILESA stayed strategically important, but the Critic successfully shifted the migration logic from 'finish the big refactor' to 'de-risk the god table through staged, access-pattern-aware cuts.'

highlights.speaker: synthesizer
highlights.message: Reliability work gained rank because several members independently identified the same bottleneck: the system is useful, but too dependent on a single operator and too exposed to platform fragility.

highlights.speaker: synthesizer
highlights.message: COAGAN's recovery model beat broader market-intelligence work because it directly informs asset protection, replanting, and future cash-flow expectations.

highlights.speaker: synthesizer
highlights.message: The Family Office OS idea survived as option value only; the council wants productizable architecture choices now, but no active commercialization until internal trust and continuity improve.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: Padel memberships and ANSA service upsell are still attractive commercial ideas, but the council judged them lower leverage than system foundations in the current moment.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: Some members wanted health data to automatically throttle workstreams now; others argued that manual stress mode should come first to avoid brittle automation and false positives.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: There remains real tension between architecturally pure DILESA normalization and pragmatic migration cuts optimized for access patterns and button safety.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: A fuller internal event bus may become important later, but the council did not believe BSOP is ready to justify that complexity today.

highlights.speaker: engineer
highlights.message: Action item: Approve a unified canonical source initiative combining registry, treaty, adapters, and reconciliation logging.

highlights.speaker: engineer
highlights.message: Action item: Choose the first 3 BSOP routes and 10-20 entities that must declare canonical source explicitly.

highlights.speaker: engineer
highlights.message: Action item: Begin DILESA Clientes migration with the documented preparation phase and make Expediente extraction the first live cut.

highlights.speaker: engineer
highlights.message: Action item: Design the first Operating Pulse around exception signals only; cap output at a small ranked action list.

recommendations.title: Canonical Source Treaty + Data Access Layer
recommendations.rationale: Establish a unified source-of-truth program: typed adapters, app-visible source registry, explicit ownership and sync rules, and reconciliation logs for dual-source entities. This creates the trust layer required for both product and operating decisions.
actionItems: Define registry fields for top entities and routes
Route selected risky pages through adapters only
Add undeclared-source logging
Publish concise DATA_TREATY.md tied to code

recommendations.title: Operating Pulse — What Needs Beto Today?
recommendations.rationale: Create a cross-business operating layer that ranks the 3-7 actions or decisions that deserve attention today, using KPI exceptions, infra health, calendar/travel context, DILESA risk, COAGAN recovery, and AI operations. It should reduce cognitive load rather than expand dashboards.
actionItems: Define a small set of exception-driven signal types
Limit output to ranked actions with source and confidence
Start from current known pain points rather than aiming for completeness

recommendations.title: DILESA Clientes Split + 3-Layer ERP Completion
recommendations.rationale: Continue DILESA's ERP transformation, but through a staged, rollback-heavy migration centered on the Clientes split, Expediente extraction, and eventual 3-layer completion. Use practical access-pattern cuts rather than pursuing schema purity for its own sake.
actionItems: Execute migration preparation plan
Start with Expediente as first low-risk cut
Stage Operaciones de Venta cutover with backups and audits
Delay deletion of legacy fields until all views and dependencies validate

recommendations.title: Solo-Operator Reliability Layer
recommendations.rationale: Build the survivability stack for BSOP: release guardrails, operator scripts, degraded-mode runbooks, and a stress/load-management policy. The goal is not elegant infrastructure but dependable operation for a single human with AI support.
actionItems: Create scripts/ops for key health and deploy checks
Document one critical degraded-mode fallback path
Add manual stress mode and define first load-shedding rules

recommendations.title: Trustworthy Metrics Layer for COAGAN and Cross-Business Review
recommendations.rationale: Start with a COAGAN tree-loss and recovery model, then build a narrow KPI layer across businesses that supports weekly review, funding, and escalation decisions. Metrics should be trusted, sparse, and action-linked.
actionItems: Model COAGAN losses and recovery scenarios by zone/block
Define 5-7 KPIs per business with owners and thresholds
Show trend plus exception plus next action only

memos.title: R&D Council Session
memos.summary: The first council session landed on a disciplined operating thesis: BSOP should become a live operating spine, not just a polished static hub, but only through staged execution. The highest-confidence first move is a live telemetry pipeline for AI Operations because it is bounded, already useful, and reusable across future modules. In parallel, the most urgent business integrity issue is RDB order deduplication and Coda table hygiene, while DILESA should be treated as the first formal modernization program rather than an ad hoc cleanup effort.

ideas.title: Convert BSOP from showcase to live operating spine
ideas.summary: Move BSOP from static credibility to operational trust by making the AI layer live first, then reusing that pattern elsewhere.

ideas.title: Build the RDB ops core around Waitry instead of replacing the whole POS
ideas.summary: Preserve what already works and build defensible control around it.

ideas.title: Treat DILESA as the flagship modernization program
ideas.summary: Sequence a serious modernization effort where the payoff is biggest.

ideas.title: Ship a normalized telemetry pipeline for OpenClaw → BSOP
ideas.summary: Build the live telemetry pipeline for AI Operations because it is bounded, already useful, and reusable across future modules.

ideas.title: Install a deterministic dedupe and archiving layer for RDB orders
ideas.summary: Create the deduplication layer for the 95 suspicious duplicate pairs found across 1,127 orders.

ideas.title: Create an executive scoreboard across ANSA, DILESA, COAGAN, and RDB
ideas.summary: Build a unified executive view across all four businesses with the metrics that matter most.

ideas.title: Do not launch three migrations at once
ideas.summary: Protect execution capacity by sequencing big moves instead of parallelizing them.

ideas.title: Use Supabase surgically, not as an excuse to rebuild everything
ideas.summary: Treat Supabase as a selective substrate, not a default answer to every problem.

ideas.title: Make BSOP the wrapper, Coda the workflow UI, and Supabase the selective data spine
ideas.summary: Define the architectural contract: BSOP wraps, Coda orchestrates workflows, Supabase holds selective data.

ideas.title: Institute a weekly council-driven operating review
ideas.summary: Convert council output into a recurring management rhythm and cross-business visibility practice.

debate.topic: BSOP as live spine vs static hub

highlights.speaker: strategist
highlights.message: BSOP needs to move from static credibility to operational trust. The AI layer should be live first, then that pattern replicates elsewhere.

highlights.speaker: engineer
highlights.message: AI Operations telemetry is the best first proving ground: raw data exists, scope is bounded, and it's reusable across future modules.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: The Strategist pushed for a big transformation; I pushed back on multi-front migration and founder overload during an active PED deal window.

highlights.speaker: analyst
highlights.message: RDB is already measurable and painful: 95 suspicious duplicate pairs across 1,127 orders. That should get attention now.

highlights.speaker: synthesizer
highlights.message: Architecture crystallized: BSOP = wrapper, Coda = workflow UI, Supabase = selective data spine.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: Travel sharing is strategically interesting but not urgent. Do not let it distract from operating leverage plays.

highlights.speaker: engineer
highlights.message: DILESA architecture work should accelerate; the Critic argued for slowing it into a formal P1 program instead.

highlights.speaker: analyst
highlights.message: Hard quantification of pain points before bigger bets. But the Strategist is right that some architectural moves are justified even before perfect measurement.

highlights.speaker: strategist
highlights.message: Shared trip links as first external-facing BSOP product is promising, but follows more urgent operating leverage plays.

recommendations.title: Build the OpenClaw → BSOP live telemetry spine first
recommendations.rationale: Lowest-risk live-data proving ground; immediate trust upgrade for BSOP AI Operations. Impact 10, effort 6, council support 9.6.
actionItems: Define normalized schema
Build scheduled sync
Add freshness/source labels to BSOP pages

recommendations.title: Harden RDB with duplicate detection, canonical orders, and archive policy
recommendations.rationale: Real quantified issue affecting daily operations; avoids premature POS rebuild. Impact 9, effort 5, council support 9.0.
actionItems: Formalize duplicate rules
Create canonical-order layer
Archive heavy raw tables on a schedule

recommendations.title: Launch DILESA as the first formal modernization program
recommendations.rationale: Highest long-term leverage across Coda-heavy systems. Impact 9, effort 8, council support 8.4.
actionItems: Rank god tables/workflows by business criticality
Define stay/archive/sync tiers
Begin with land → anteproyectos → proyectos flow

recommendations.title: Create a weekly executive scoreboard across the four businesses
recommendations.rationale: Converts council output into management rhythm and cross-business visibility. Impact 9, effort 6, council support 8.8.
actionItems: Pick 5–8 KPIs total
Assign source + owner + freshness mode
Publish v1 even if partially manual

recommendations.title: Use shared trip links and expense settlement as BSOP's first outside-user surface
recommendations.rationale: Best low-risk collaborative use case, but not the most urgent operational leverage. Impact 7, effort 4, council support 7.2.
actionItems: Finish trip detail polish and mobile validation
Add balances/category/FX transparency
Use Taos as the real-world test case
